title: × Citrofortunella microcarpa
taxonomy: I found that × Citrofortunella microcarpa is a hybrid of citrus plants. It belongs to the family Rutaceae and is commonly known as the Calamondin or Philippine lime. The classification of this plant is quite specific, as it is a cross between the orange (Citrus sinensis) and the kumquat (Fortunella spp.). This hybrid is often used as an ornamental plant or for its edible fruit, which is sour and can be used in cooking. The classification of × Citrofortunella microcarpa as a hybrid of citrus plants highlights its unique characteristics and properties.
uses: × Citrofortunella microcarpa, also known as the calamansi or Philippine lime, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant's leaves and fruits are rich in antioxidants and have been used to treat digestive issues, such as nausea and diarrhea. Its antimicrobial properties have also been utilized to treat skin infections and wounds. Additionally, the plant has been used to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels due to its flavonoid content. In some cultures, the plant's leaves are also used to treat fever and respiratory issues.

constituents: × Citrofortunella microcarpa, also known as the Calamondin or Philippine lime, contains active constituents that contribute to its medicinal properties. parts: × Citrofortunella microcarpa, also known as the Calamondin, is a citrus plant that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. preparations: × Citrofortunella microcarpa is not a commonly recognized or widely used species name for a plant. However, the related plant Fortunella (a type of kumquat) is used in herbal preparations. In traditional medicine, the fruit, bark, and leaves of Fortunella species are used to make infusions and decoctions for various purposes, including as a digestive aid and to treat colds and fever.
